content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660 322-5901 322-6444

November 3, 1976

Ms. Pauline Soules, Secretary
Ruth Lake Community Services District
Star Route Box 411
Bridgeville, CA 95526

Dear Ms. Soules:

Please be advised that the deadline date for the submission of
a Conflict of Interest Code of the Ruth Lake Community Services
District to the Fair Political Practices Commission is February 10,
1977. The District was inadvertently omitted from the list of
agencies with jurisdiction in more than one county supplied to
this office by the Office of the Controller from which we comprised
our mailing list.

Local government agencies with jurisdiction in more than one
county must develop Conflict of Interest Codes pursuant to
Government Code Section 87300, et seq. for submission to the Fair
Political Practices Commission.

Attached is an information package for the development of
your Conflict of Interest Codes pursuant to Government Code
Section 87300, et seq.

I am informed by Mr. John L. Loomis, Assistant District
Attorney that your's is a very small District which would in all
probability designate only the Directors for inclusion within
the Code. Under such circumstances, you may wish to seriously
consider recommending the adoption of a "Small District Code" as
set forth in Section VI.A.3 of the informational materials
package.

If you have any questions regarding the District's obligation
under the Political Reform Act, please do not hesitate to call
this office.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division

DLS:mra
cc: John L. Loomis, Asst. District Attorney

Enclosure

sections.question_synthetic: What is the deadline for Ruth Lake Community Services District to submit a Conflict of Interest Code to the FPPC, and what are the requirements for local government agencies with multi-county jurisdiction?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that Ruth Lake Community Services District must submit its Conflict of Interest Code by February 10, 1977, pursuant to Government Code Section 87300, and recommends the District consider adopting a 'Small District Code' given its size.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an administrative notification letter without traditional QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. The letter contains procedural guidance and a deadline notification rather than a response to a specific legal inquiry. Some text is partially illegible due to document quality, but core information is extractable.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What is the deadline for Ruth Lake Community Services District to submit a Conflict of Interest Code to the FPPC, and what are the requirements for local government agencies with multi-county jurisdiction?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advises that Ruth Lake Community Services District must submit its Conflict of Interest Code by February 10, 1977, pursuant to Government Code Section 87300, and recommends the District consider adopting a 'Small District Code' given its size.

Enforcement/Conflict of Interest (916) 322-5660 322-5901 322-6444 November 3, 1976 Ms. Pauline Soules, Secretary Ruth Lake Community Services District Star Route Box 411 Bridgeville, CA 95526 Dear Ms. Soules: Please be advised that the deadline date for the submission of a Conflict of Interest Code of the Ruth Lake Community Services District to the Fair Political Practices Commission is February 10, 1977. The District was inadvertently omitted from the list of agencies with jurisdiction in more than one county supplied to this office by the Office of the Controller from which we comprised our mailing list. Local government agencies with jurisdiction in more than one county must develop Conflict of Interest Codes pursuant to Government Code Section 87300, et seq. for submission to the Fair Political Practices Commission. Attached is an information package for the development of your Conflict of Interest Codes pursuant to Government Code Section 87300, et seq. I am informed by Mr. John L. Loomis, Assistant District Attorney that your's is a very small District which would in all probability designate only the Directors for inclusion within the Code. Under such circumstances, you may wish to seriously consider recommending the adoption of a "Small District Code" as set forth in Section VI.A.3 of the informational materials package. If you have any questions regarding the District's obligation under the Political Reform Act, please do not hesitate to call this office. Sincerely, [signature] Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief Conflicts of Interest Division DLS:mra cc: John L. Loomis, Asst. District Attorney Enclosure
embedding.summary: The FPPC notifies Ruth Lake Community Services District of its obligation to develop and submit a Conflict of Interest Code by February 10, 1977, and provides guidance on compliance with Government Code Section 87300 for multi-county jurisdictional agencies.
